title: kNN in Action: Recommendation and Classification
concept: Real-world applications of kNN: recommending similar items, evaluating credit risk, and predicting numeric values by averaging neighbors.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What are two main limitations of the kNN algorithm?

options.text: It is slow on large datasets and is sensitive to irrelevant features.
It has a long training time and cannot perform classification.
It requires mathematical formulas and only works with 1 neighbor.

exercises.explain: Correct! kNN requires calculating all distances at prediction time, making it slow on big data, and it is sensitive to feature scaling.

cards.title: A map of taste
cards.body: How does a streaming app know what movie you want to watch next? It doesn't need to read your mind. It just maps your tastes on a coordinate grid alongside other users. If your grid coordinates are right next to 5 other people's, we can recommend whatever they liked. This is kNN in action!

cards.title: kNN applications
cards.body: kNN is widely used in the real world for similarity-based recommendations (finding items close to what you like) and simple classification tasks (sorting customers or risks based on past profiles).

cards.title: Evaluating loans
cards.body: When a bank decides whether to approve a loan, it maps the applicant's profile (income, job history, debt) on a grid. If the applicant's 7 closest neighbor profiles on the grid all paid back their loans successfully, the bank approves the loan!

cards.title: Product matching
cards.body: E-commerce sites map items by attributes like price, brand, and category. When you view a shirt, the site uses kNN with k = 4 to pull the 4 "closest" shirts on the grid and displays them as "similar items you might like".

cards.title: kNN predicts numbers
cards.body: You might think kNN is only for classification (sorting into groups). But it can predict numbers (regression) too! Instead of letting neighbors vote on a category, kNN can average their values. To estimate a house price, it finds the 5 closest homes and averages their prices!

cards.title: How kNN acts
cards.body: Two ways kNN is applied in business:

left.text:
Finds the closest users or items
on the grid to recommend matching
tastes or products.

right.text:
Checks if a new profile is near
mostly safe profiles or mostly
risky profiles.

exercises.prompt: How does kNN recommend a movie to you?

options.text: By mapping your profile on a grid and recommending movies watched by your closest coordinate neighbors.
By randomly picking a movie from the top 100 chart.
By running a search for the word 'movie' in its database.

exercises.explain: Exactly! kNN maps users or items in a coordinate space and finds the nearest neighbors to recommend matching items.

exercises.prompt: True or False: kNN can be used to predict continuous numeric values (like a house price) by averaging the values of the k nearest neighbors.
exercises.explain: True! For regression tasks, kNN takes the average value of the neighbors instead of a majority vote.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence about credit classification:
exercises.sentence: A bank maps loan applicants on a grid. If a new applicant's closest neighbors are all safe borrowers, kNN classifies the applicant as ___.
exercises.explain: Since the majority of closest neighbors are safe borrowers, the new point is classified as safe.

exercises.prompt: Which of these represents a kNN regression prediction?

options.text: A house price is estimated at $350k by taking the average price of the 5 closest homes sold nearby.
A house is classified as 'Modern' because a human agent marked it so.
A house is listed for sale because the owner needs to relocate.

exercises.explain: Correct! Averaging the values of the nearest neighbors is how kNN is used for regression.

exercises.prompt: You run a streaming platform. You want to recommend a new video to Sarah. You map Sarah on a grid based on her music tastes. Her 3 closest neighbors are John (loves Jazz), Mike (loves Jazz), and Anna (loves Rock). If k = 3, what category of video will kNN recommend to Sarah?

options.text: Jazz, because it has the majority vote (2 out of 3 closest neighbors).
Rock, because Anna is the single closest neighbor.
A mixture of Jazz and Rock played at the same time.
Pop, because it is the most popular genre overall on the app.

exercises.explain: Incredible! Sarah's neighbors vote: 2 for Jazz (John and Mike) and 1 for Rock (Anna). By majority vote, Jazz wins. kNN successfully matches her with the tastes of her closest neighbors!
